feedback.text: Hallo! Video games are under threat of attack by activists. A new group of people has put credit card companies such as VISA under pressure to get certain games from online distributors completely away from the online shop, and it even removes the games of people who have already bought it. The credit card companies claim that they do not make decisions themselves and simply follow the law, but the evidence that games are being removed from online shops on a massive scale and the activist groups who recently claim that they are successful with their aims is enough evidence to the contrary, let alone that many people really no longer have their games. This is very anti-consumer behaviour and many people like me playing dollars in their free time would like to see something about this. And that we want something against this is a fact, evidence of this is the Stop Killing Games initiative: A major campaign to ensure that video games can no longer completely remove a game from companies such as Ubisoft. An initiative that we can sign up to 31 July 2025 has so far more than 1.4 million people who have signed. Many of us want this to be addressed, and if nothing happens, more people become even more sip, deception, and richer.
